---
title: "Analysis Playbook"
subtitle: "Business questions, answered with the right analysis, the right chart, and the correct read"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.section-label}
Visualization & Measurement Library
:::

Every entry in this playbook starts with a question someone actually asks in a business —
*did the campaign change the trajectory? is this lift real or noise? which metric should
we track?* — and pairs it with the analysis that answers it honestly, the R code that
builds it, and the statistical read that goes with it. Where it's instructive, the wrong
chart is shown next to the right one.

All data is synthetic and seeded, engineered to demonstrate exactly the pattern each
entry is about.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**🚧 In progress** — entries are being added across the five categories below.
:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Category 01]{.fc-category}
[In progress]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Trends & Time Series](trends/index.qmd){.stretched-link}

Trend breaks, seasonality, dynamic baselines, and anomalies — how to show whether a
trajectory actually changed, and how to separate signal from ordinary variance.

:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Category 02]{.fc-category}
[In progress]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Comparison](comparison/index.qmd){.stretched-link}

Share shift, mix effects, and before/after reads — comparing groups, periods, and
segments without letting the comparison itself mislead.

:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Category 03]{.fc-category}
[In progress]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Distributions & Uncertainty](distributions/index.qmd){.stretched-link}

Why the average lies — showing variance honestly, when the mean hides the story, and
how to put uncertainty on a chart without losing the room.

:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Category 04]{.fc-category}
[In progress]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Experiment Readouts](experiments/index.qmd){.stretched-link}

Test vs. control done right — lift with confidence intervals, significance reads, and
the difference between "the test won" and "the test probably won."

:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Category 05]{.fc-category}
[In progress]{.fc-data}
:::

### [KPIs & Metrics](kpis/index.qmd){.stretched-link}

Leading vs. lagging indicators, vanity vs. trackable metrics — how to define a KPI
that survives a quarter of scrutiny, and how to present it.

:::
